note: Implementer (Robotics) example for OPC UA - OpenUSD Bindings. It binds an OPC 40010 MotionDeviceSystem (a robot cell of two 6-axis articulated robots) to an OpenUSD scene and drives the robots' joints live from each Axis' ActualPosition. It exercises the composition model recursively: the system is composed of MotionDevices (1..n), each MotionDevice is composed of Axes, and each Axis representation drives one joint. This descriptor is the machine-readable source of truth; it is mirrored by the C# RoboticsDeviceIntegrationServer sample (RobotOpenUsdE2eTests) and by usd_writer.py, and it matches the geometry in robot.usda / Cell.usda. Browse paths are relative to the represented Object; a connector discovers SourceNodeId directly via Server/OpenUSD/Representations and does not need these paths.

stage.rootLayerDigestNote: The server publishes RootLayerDigest = SHA-256 over the resolved root layer (a Twin-BOM content-integrity check). The demo digests the rootLayerIdentifier as a deterministic stand-in; a connector verifies it before composing the stage.

servedAssets.note: Optional OU-AssetDelivery capability: the server serves these USD layers through <Stage>.Assets (Part 5 FileType); a connector fetches + verifies + caches them and composes the stage locally, no external asset repo needed.

kinematics.note: 6-axis articulated arm. The link Xforms are nested in robot.usda so each joint's local rotate composes onto the previous link (a serial kinematic chain). rotationAxis picks the xformOp the Axis' ActualPosition drives; angles are in degrees (USD rotate ops are degrees, so the connector applies them directly). linkPrimPath is relative to the robot representation PrimPath.

articulationBinding.note: One articulation binding lives on EACH Axis representation. Its target prim is the Axis' link Xform and its target property is the link's rotate op (from kinematics.axisTemplate). ActualPosition (deg) maps 1:1 to the rotate angle (deg).

representations.note: The registry (Server/OpenUSD/Representations) holds 15 representations for the cell: 1 system + 2 robots + 12 axes. A generic connector processes every one and assembles the tree depth-first (base spec §5.12 Nesting).

components.name: RobotsAggregation
components.note: 1..n composition (§5.12): the cell aggregates its MotionDevices. Reference (NOT Instance) is used deliberately so each robot keeps its own overridable copy of the kinematic chain and articulates independently; instanceable prims would share a single joint pose across both robots.

bindings.name: EmergencyStopBeacon
bindings.note: Cell-wide emergency-stop drives the safety beacon visibility ('inherited' when the e-stop is active, 'invisible' when clear).

bindings.name: SpeedOverrideCommand
bindings.note: Opt-in control (§5.10): a USD-side speed-override intent (0..100 %) is written to the controller SpeedOverride Variable. Declared (enabled=true) and Controllable, but a connector actuates it only when explicitly enabled (bridge --enable-commands) AND authorized - single-writer, fail-closed.

robots.browseNameNote: The MotionDevice Object BrowseName is 'R1' (not 'Robot_R1') because the generic connector composes each 1..n instance prim as <TargetPrimPath>/<BrowseName>; 'R1' yields /Cell/Robots/R1, matching this representation's PrimPath and the robot.usda reference site.

components.name: AxesAggregation
components.note: The robot is composed of its 6 Axes. Child arc: each Axis maps to its (pre-authored) link Xform inside robot.usda; each Axis' own representation then drives that link's joint (recursive nesting).

components.name: GripperTool
components.note: Dynamic composition (§5.13): a gripper tool is mounted on R1's flange at runtime. When the server adds the MountedTool node it emits a GeneralModelChange event; the connector composes the Tool reference prim. On removal the connector authors active=false.

bindings.name: EmergencyStopWarning
bindings.note: A warning halo on the robot becomes visible while the cell emergency-stop is active ('inherited') and hidden otherwise ('invisible'). Visibility (a Boolean->token mapping) is used rather than a colour because the shared EmergencyStop source is Boolean; the generic connector maps a non-zero source to 'inherited'. The source is the shared cell EmergencyStop, resolved relative to the MotionDeviceSystem.

components.name: AxesAggregation
components.note: R2 is composed of its 6 Axes (same template as R1); it carries no tool.

bindings.name: EmergencyStopWarning
bindings.note: R2 warning halo becomes visible while the cell emergency-stop is active.

representations.axesNote: Each of the 12 Axis Objects (6 per robot) has its own OpenUsdRepresentation whose PrimPath is the Axis' link Xform (robot PrimPath + kinematics.axisTemplate[i].linkPrimPath) and which carries one articulationBinding whose TargetPropertyName is that axis' rotateOp. bindingDefinitionId for an axis articulation binding = 'c<robot>-axis-<name>' (deterministic), e.g. axis A3 on R1 -> the connector key is (R1.Axes.A3, articulation).

simulation.note: The server animates the joints so the demo visibly moves. Each robot follows a smooth sinusoidal sweep about the home pose; R2 is phase-shifted from R1 so the two arms move independently (proving the Reference (non-instanced) aggregation). EmergencyStop toggles a few times to exercise the alarm + dynamic reconciliation; the gripper tool is attached to R1 at runtime.
